steps.title: Write the intersection equation
steps.content:
The graphs intersect where the two expressions for $y$ are equal:
$$x-c=-5(x-10)^2$$
Expanding and moving everything to one side gives
$$5x^2-99x+(500-c)=0$$

steps.title: Require two real solutions
steps.content:
Two distinct real solutions require a positive discriminant:
$$99^2-4(5)(500-c)>0$$
$$9{,}801-10{,}000+20c>0$$
$$20c-199>0$$
$$c>\frac{199}{20}$$

steps.title: Choose the listed value
steps.content: The value $$\frac{199}{20}$$ gives one repeated solution, not two distinct solutions. Among the choices, only $20$ is greater than $$\frac{199}{20}$$ The answer is D.
